items.title: UK Navy Says Ship in Gulf of Oman Taken, Heading to Iran
items.summary: A UK naval group reported a commercial vessel was taken by unauthorized personnel at the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz and is now bound for Iranian waters. The ship was taken 38 nautical miles off the UAE in the Gulf of Oman where it was at anchor, the UK Maritime Trade Operations said in a statement. Bloomberg’s Anthony di Paola has more.

items.title: US Says China Wants More Of Its Oil to Cut Middle East Reliance
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping opposes efforts to charge a toll to navigate the Strait of Hormuz and is interested in purchasing more US oil to reduce its dependence on the waterway in the future, according to a White House official.

items.title: US Efforts to End Iran War Stumble as Ship Seized Near UAE
items.summary: US efforts to end the war with Iran were dealt a setback after a commercial vessel was apparently seized by unauthorized personnel near the United Arab Emirates, increasing uncertainty over control of the critical Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump-Xi Summit: Lavish Welcome, Taiwan in Focus
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned US President Donald Trump of a potential conflict if the Taiwan issue is mismanaged, in blunt remarks that punctured an otherwise cordial start to the first trip to China by a sitting US president in nearly a decade. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.summary: Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins. items.title: Martin Wolf on the 'Terrifying' Superpower the US Wields | Odd Lots
items.summary: Last year, when we talked to Martin Wolf, the global order seemed like it was being upended after President Trump unveiled his sweeping tariffs against nearly every US trading partner. A lot has happened since then. items.summary: Last year, when we talked to Martin Wolf, the global order seemed like it was being upended after President Trump unveiled his sweeping tariffs against nearly every US trading partner. A lot has happened since then. In fact, April 2025 seems almost quaint when compared to 2026 so far, from the Supreme Court’s tariff ruling to the US-Israel war with Iran. items.title: Geely, Xiaomi lead the pack: battery EVs top China sales on rising oil costs
items.summary: Surging oil prices are prompting Chinese buyers to shift to battery electric vehicles (BEVs), which dominated April sales rankings by taking nine out of the top 10 spots – a new high in China’s electrification drive. Only one petrol car model was among the bestsellers across all power trains, with Geely’s Binyue in eighth place on sales of 14,923 units, according to data from automotive platform D

items.title: UN Climate Chief Lavishes Praise on China as Trump Meets Xi
items.summary: China is reaping benefits as a global leader on the green transition and other nations should follow as the Iran war shows the vulnerability of a fossil fuel-based economy, according to the top United Nations climate official.

items.title: Cuba says oil and diesel supplies have run dry under U.S. sanctions
items.summary: A Cuban official said the situation is “extremely tense” as blackouts sparked protests in Havana on Wednesday.

items.title: Ship Taken in Gulf of Oman and Heading to Iran, UK Navy Says
items.summary: A UK naval group reported a commercial vessel was taken by unauthorized personnel at the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz and is now bound for Iranian waters, the latest indication of Tehran exerting control on shipping in the region.

items.title: Xi Tells US CEOs Accompanying Trump That China Will Open Up More
items.summary: Xi Jinping signaled China is moving toward greater openness, striking an upbeat note during his meeting with US business leaders accompanying Donald Trump as the two presidents wrapped up morning talks that featured more divisive issues like trade and Taiwan.

items.title: Adtek Is Said to Explore Hong Kong IPO at $4 Billion Valuation
items.summary: Shenzhen Adtek Technology Co. is considering an initial public offering in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a wave of Chinese listings by businesses related to data centers and artificial intelligence.

items.title: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai Profit Jumps With AI Sales Boost
items.summary: Nvidia Corp.’s major server assembly partner Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a stronger-than-expected increase in quarterly profit, highlighting sustained spending on hardware essential for AI.

items.title: Cerebras Raises $5.55 Billion in Year’s Biggest IPO
items.summary: Artificial intelligence chipmaker Cerebras Systems Inc. raised $5.55 billion in its US initial public offering. The company priced its shares at $185 each, giving Cerebras a market value of about $40 billion, based on the shares offered and outstanding in its filings. Bloomberg’s Neil Campling reports.

items.title: Treasury repo investments could be another route to a slightly smaller Fed balance sheet
items.summary: The US Treasury has proposed investing some of the idle cash it keeps at the Federal Reserve into money markets

items.title: Tech groups score win on clean energy rules for gas-powered data centres
items.summary: Corporate climate watchdog drops stricter proposal on net zero claims after heavy lobbying

items.title: Franklin CEO Says Markets Are Underestimating Inflation
items.summary: Franklin Templeton Chief Executive Officer Jenny Johnson says that the US economy is "still very healthy" and there are reasons for the "euphoria" around markets. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Johnson also warns of inflation risks, saying that "the reality of inflation is it's sticky" and markets are underestimating the reality of it. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Oil rises as IEA flags greater volatility ahead, OPEC cuts demand forecast
items.summary: OPEC cut its demand growth estimates for 2026 to about 1.2 million barrels per day.

items.title: War disruption grows and data centres go to space
items.summary: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

items.title: UK Posts Strongest Growth in a Year Even as Headwinds Build
items.summary: Panmure Liberum economist Simon French discusses the UK economy and the political crisis surrounding Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Gross domestic product grew 0.6% in the first quarter, a jump from the 0.2% in the previous three months and the fastest expansion for a year. French speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: 'Better Than Ever': Trump, Xi Hail Ties at Beijing Summit | The China Show 5/14/2026
items.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Yvonne Man give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Seeks $2 Billion Loan for Malaysia Growth
items.summary: Blackstone Inc.-owned data center firm AirTrunk is marketing a $2.3 billion loan to fund a Malaysia project, according to people familiar with the matter, part of a slew of tech-linked financings in Southeast Asia spurred by the artificial intelligence boom.

items.title: Cerebras prices IPO above expected range, as Wall Street braces for AI tsunami
items.summary: Cerebras raised $5.55 billion in its IPO, and with the chipmaker's offering, investors are gearing up for some even bigger AI deals later this year.

items.title: Blackstone REIT Raises $1.75 Billion in IPO to Buy Data Centers
items.summary: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Inc. raised $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering, as investors’ appetite for artificial intelligence infrastructure shows no sign of easing.

items.title: Cisco's stock pops 17% on surging AI orders, as company says it's cutting almost 4,000 jobs
items.summary: Cisco's AI story has finally started resonating with Wall Street, with the stock hitting a record late last year and continuing to rally in 2026.

items.title: Cuba has run out of diesel and oil, energy minister says
items.summary: The minister says the situation in Cuba is "extremely tense", as a US-led blockade of oil to the country causes widespread power cuts.

items.title: Alibaba shares surge 7% in Hong Kong as firm accelerates pivot to AI
items.summary: Shares of Alibaba Group Holding opened up 7.8 per cent in Hong Kong, after the firm reported strong growth from its artificial intelligence products and ramped up its commitment to the technology. The Chinese tech giant also saw its New York-listed shares surge 8.2 per cent on Wednesday, closing at US$145.81, after it confirmed that revenues from AI-related products had hit 8.97 billion yuan (US$1

items.title: Australian giant Coles misled shoppers with fake discounts, court rules
items.summary: The decision, which comes amid similar case against Woolworths, could mean significant penalties.

items.summary: Kevin Warsh received the fewest votes of any Fed chair in history. Will it cripple him?

items.title: Kevin Warsh wins Senate confirmation as the next Federal Reserve chair
items.summary: In the most divisive vote ever for a Fed chair, Warsh, 56, won confirmation to take over for Jerome Powell.

items.title: Hong Kong’s Central office rents set to reverse multi-year slump with gains in second half
items.summary: Office rents in Hong Kong’s Central district are expected to rise faster in the second quarter of the year, according to analysts, a reversal of a multi-year slump that began in the second half of 2019. The latest forecast was made by US bank Citi, which said premium offices in the city’s main business zone had seen rents rise 1.7 per cent from a trough in October, with room for further increments

items.title: Shares surge in debut of power producer favoured by Trump energy agenda
items.summary: Geothermal generator Fervo uses renewable energy technology that has garnered rare administration support

items.summary: Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the narrowest margin since the role required a Senate confirmation vote.

items.title: Mortgage rates jump to highest level since March on hotter inflation reports
items.summary: Mortgage rates surged to the highest level since March in reaction to two hotter-than-expected inflation reports.

items.title: 'Bond markets on edge' as King Charles sets out fragile UK government's agenda
items.summary: U.K. gilts and bonds faced heavy selling pressure in response to the latest 'Starmer drama', as the prime minister faced down rivals ahead of the King's Speech.

items.title: Tesla lures budget-conscious Chinese buyers with cheap loans ahead of state visit by Trump
items.summary: Tesla China has launched a new financing scheme to attract more budget-conscious buyers after its sales in the world’s largest electric vehicle (EV) market fell behind its domestic rivals. The US carmaker said on Wednesday that the down payment for a Shanghai-made Model 3 vehicle – priced at 235,500 yuan (US$34,672) – would be slashed to 55,900 yuan from 79,900 yuan if the buyer chose a five-year

items.title: Hong Kong’s first biodiversity loan goes to Henderson Land for Central Yards green spaces
items.summary: Henderson Land Development secured the first biodiversity loan in Hong Kong, receiving around HK$100 million (US$12.8 million) from HSBC and Hang Seng Bank for green initiatives at Central Yards, the company’s flagship mixed-use development on the New Central Harbourfront.
